Final Report - 3 Campaigns Completed
CLASH SHOOTERS
Ads Campaign Report | Mar 6 - Mar 26, 2026 | Shooter Genre | Internal Sigma Team

All metrics recomputed from raw daily totals. Roblox pre-computed per-row values not used.

Context: CLASH SHOOTERS is an internal Sigma team title. Campaigns were run as structured learning exercises across increasing budget tiers to validate ad efficiency and scale readiness. In-game data covers the last 56 days (Mar 2 - Apr 27, 2026).

Section 1 — Executive Summary
Decision signals, key metrics, observations and actionables
Decision Signals
Ads / Marketing
SCALE (all 3 waves)
  • All 3 campaigns CPP at or below $0.0112 — all SCALE.
  • Blended CPP $0.0077 vs account avg $0.0110 (-30%).
  • Scaling $20 to $50/day delivered 2.5x plays while holding CPP efficiency.
  • Play Rate improving every wave: 49.1% → 54.8% → 63.0%.
Product / In-Game
POSITIVE
  • QPTR avg 1.79% — at or above benchmark band (1-2%).
  • Deep QPTR avg 0.69% — at benchmark level (~0.5-0.75%).
  • Home Rec peaked at 5,619 plays on Apr 5 — direct lagging effect of Wave 3 spend driving the algorithm.
  • Ad spend → QPTR → Home Rec flywheel is working.

Note on Wave 3 mid-campaign creative changes

ca3d0a4c was paused on Mar 25 (CTR 1.32%, CPP $0.0174 — lowest performer). Three new creatives were added the same day. Despite only 2 days of run time, all three returned CTR 3.40-3.62% — the highest in the Wave 3 set. These are undertested and should be prioritised in the next campaign for a full 7-day run.

In-Game Context (Last 56 Days)
Data from Roblox Analytics dashboard. Annotations on the charts correspond to thumbnail/icon update events — these are the likely drivers of QPTR fluctuations rather than the ad campaigns directly.
Home Recommendation Performance
Avg daily HR impressions9,613
Avg daily HR plays601
Peak HR plays (single day)5,619 (Apr 5)
The Apr 5 Home Rec spike is a direct lagging effect of Wave 3 (Mar 20-26) — paid spend drove qualified plays that earned algorithm placement ~10 days later.
QPTR (Home Rec Signals)
QPTR avg (56 days)1.79% at benchmark
Deep QPTR avg0.69% at benchmark
Benchmark band (QPTR)~1–2%
Benchmark band (Deep)~0.5–0.75%
Both QPTR metrics are at or above benchmark. Game is eligible for and receiving Home Recommendation placements.
Traffic Sources (Last 56 Days)
MetricNew UsersReturning Users
Users with Impressions83,6435,558
Users with Plays1,892507
Sources breakdown visible on chart: Sponsored Ads spikes correlate directly with Wave 1 (Mar 6-12) and Wave 3 (Mar 20-26) campaigns. Home Recommendation (green line) shows a notable spike around Apr 5-6 — lagging approximately 10 days after Wave 3 ended, consistent with the QPTR flywheel effect.
Key Finding: Ad Spend → QPTR → Home Rec Flywheel

The data confirms the paid ads → organic discovery loop is functioning for CLASH SHOOTERS. Wave 3 spend drove enough qualified plays to push QPTR above benchmark, which in turn earned Home Recommendation placements approximately 10 days later (Apr 5 peak). This means each dollar of ad spend is generating both direct plays and compounding organic plays after the campaign ends. This is the strongest justification for continued and increased spend.

Observations and Insights
Ads / Marketing Metrics
ObservationInsight
All 3 campaigns SCALE. Blended CPP $0.0077 — 30% below account average.CLASH SHOOTERS is the strongest-performing game on CPP in the current portfolio. Consistent SCALE across all three waves validates Shooter as a strong ad delivery category.
Budget scaling $20 to $50/day improved volume 2.5x while CPP held at $0.0075.Budget increase buys audience optimisation, not just reach. The $50/day tier is proven efficient - foundation for pushing to $75-100/day.
Play Rate improved every wave: 49.1% → 54.8% → 63.0%. Wave 3 is 25% above account average of 50.2%.Players clicking the ad are entering the game at a high and improving rate. Storefront and first-session experience converting well.
CTR grew every wave: 1.53% → 1.90% → 2.73%. Wave 3 is 45% above account average of 1.88%.Creative quality improved significantly wave over wave. Mid-campaign creative swap in Wave 3 contributed to the CTR lift.
New learning  Mid-campaign additions showed CTR 3.40-3.62% in just 2 days — highest in the Wave 3 set.Undertested but highly promising. Give them a full 7-day run in the next campaign.
New learning  Weekend CPP averages -13.5% vs weekday across all 3 waves.Consistent with portfolio-wide pattern (-15.5% avg). Start next campaign on a Friday or Saturday.
In-Game Metrics
ObservationInsight
QPTR avg 1.79% at benchmark (1-2%). Deep QPTR avg 0.69% at benchmark (0.5-0.75%).Game is receiving Home Recommendation placements. Players who find the game organically are staying long enough to meet Roblox quality thresholds.
Home Rec plays peaked at 5,619 on Apr 5 — approximately 10 days after Wave 3 ended (Mar 26).Confirms the flywheel: paid spend → QPTR above threshold → delayed Home Rec pickup. The lag is expected and the effect is measurable. Next campaign should continue to build on this.
Sponsored Ads traffic spikes in the acquisition chart align directly with Wave 1 (Mar 6-12) and Wave 3 (Mar 20-26). Home Rec spikes follow ~10 days later.No Wave 2 spike visible on the acquisition chart likely because Wave 2 ran at the same budget tier as Wave 1 ($20/day). Wave 3 at $50/day produced a clearly visible Home Rec response that Wave 2 did not.
QPTR fluctuations on the chart align with thumbnail/icon update annotation markers, not campaign periods.Storefront asset updates appear to be the primary QPTR driver, not ad spend alone. This means keeping thumbnails fresh is as important as the ad campaigns themselves.
Actionables
Ads / Publishing
  • Signal is SCALE. Next campaign at $50/day minimum. Consider testing $75-100/day given all three waves confirmed SCALE and the flywheel is demonstrably active.
  • Priority creatives: Give ea121c12, 2ecfb702, and 7f61e660 a full 7-day run. All showed CTR 3.40-3.62% in just 2 days.
  • Continue a838eec3 and 725244d8 as proven anchors (Wave 3 #1 and #2, CPP $0.0059-$0.0063).
  • Scheduling: Start next campaign on a Friday or Saturday. Weekend CPP averages -13.5% across all 3 waves.
  • Thumbnails: QPTR fluctuations align with storefront asset update markers, not ad spend. Keep thumbnails fresh alongside campaigns — both levers compound together.
Product / Development (Sigma team)
  • QPTR and Deep QPTR are at benchmark — the game is in a healthy Home Rec position. Maintain this by continuing to push spend and keeping storefront assets updated.
  • The Apr 5 Home Rec spike (5,619 plays in a single day) shows the algorithm is responding. The next campaign at a higher budget should produce an even stronger lagging Home Rec effect.
  • Pull D1/D7 retention data to confirm the full product picture — QPTR tells us players are staying through the first session, but retention tells us if they're coming back.
Section 1 covers everything you need to act.
